name: Resolution Pathway and Tissue Repair Coverage Assessment

assessment_window.basis: Delta surveillance from the published baseline event through the current surveillance pass.

summary: Coverage is adequate for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public baseline. The support map includes aged-animal RvD1, MaR1, and nano-proresolving tissue-repair evidence; human SPM-enriched fish-oil and SPM-active bioavailability or target-engagement studies; EPA/DHA chronic venous leg ulcer biomarker and wound-fluid studies; and registry monitoring for the larger older-adult CVLU trial. Coverage is not strong because direct human RvD1 or MaR1 tissue-repair efficacy, definitive human wound-repair outcomes, chronic safety and durability, and posted results from NCT03576989 remain missing.

evidence_categories.rationale: Human interventional evidence covers SPM-enriched marine oil target engagement, SPM-active bioavailability in obesity, and EPA/DHA chronic venous leg ulcer inflammatory or wound-fluid markers, but not definitive human tissue-repair efficacy.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The human evidence supports target engagement and inflammatory-resolution markers, not direct repair efficacy for RvD1, MaR1, or direct SPM therapy.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current public claim is intervention-stage and does not rely on observational or natural-experiment evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Observational wound, inflammatory-resolution, or SPM-biomarker studies could provide context but would not independently upgrade the stage.

evidence_categories.rationale: Aged-animal repair evidence anchors the direct RvD1, MaR1, and nano-proresolving branches, including aged inflammatory-resolution deficits, muscle injury recovery, and fracture healing.
evidence_categories.gap_note: These are tissue-repair healthspan models rather than lifespan studies, and they do not establish human efficacy.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes specialized pro-resolving mediator biology, EPA/DHA precursor relationships, leukocyte phenotype and phagocytosis changes, cytokine and PMN activation markers, and aged-resolution impairment mechanisms.

The public outlook and evidence reviews explicitly capture the main limitations: direct SPM repair is animal-level, human evidence is biomarker-focused, EPA/DHA precursor therapy is not interchangeable with direct RvD1 or MaR1 delivery, and the larger older-adult CVLU trial has no posted results.
evidence_categories.gap_note: No definitive negative larger trial result is available yet; the strongest limiting record is still absence of posted outcomes.

evidence_categories.rationale: Short-term human fish-oil, SPM-active, and EPA/DHA studies provide some tolerability context, but direct SPM human safety, frail or older wound-population safety, chronic durability, and product-specific safety boundaries are weak.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Longer direct-SPM and older-adult wound-population safety data remain important watch items.

evidence_categories.rationale: Current surveillance rechecked the baseline registries. NCT03347006 and NCT04701138 have posted results, NCT02719665 remains completed without results, and NCT03576989 remains completed without posted results but with official unposted-results submission activity.
evidence_categories.gap_note: NCT03576989 posted results are the highest-priority active-trial gap.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline is built primarily from primary sources and registries. It does not yet include a recent systematic review or meta-analysis specifically integrating SPMs, EPA/DHA, aging, and human tissue repair.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A future coverage repair could add review-level triangulation if the track advances beyond biomarker-stage claims.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes major direct aged-animal RvD1, MaR1, and resolution-deficit anchors plus representative human target-engagement and CVLU biomarker anchors.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A deeper historical SPM-discovery and repair-biology audit could improve strong-level coverage, but is not required for the current low-confidence baseline.

evidence_categories.rationale: The outlook and evidence reviews explicitly separate direct RvD1 and MaR1, SPM-enriched marine oil, SPM-active products, and EPA/DHA precursor therapy, and they keep CVLU wound evidence distinct from broad LEV tissue repair claims.

search_log_summary: Delta surveillance checked PubMed same-window result queries, PubMed NCT/title lookup for NCT03576989, direct ClinicalTrials.gov records for NCT03576989, NCT03347006, NCT04701138, and NCT02719665, plus a broader ClinicalTrials.gov search for pro-resolving mediator and omega-3 wound or repair trials. No public-update material was found.
source_selection_notes: PMID 31948466 was excluded as the 2020 NCT03576989 protocol rather than a new results paper.
NCT03576989 remains public no-results context and should be watched because the official record shows unposted-results submission activity.
NCT01747447 and NCT06036485 were excluded as wrong-scope omega-3-related registries.
No new same-window PubMed result changed the current support map.

known_gaps.description: The completed older-adult chronic venous leg ulcer EPA/DHA trial has no posted results, despite official unposted-results submission and reset activity.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Recheck NCT03576989 and PubMed for posted results or linked publications during the next surveillance pass.

known_gaps.description: No human study yet tests direct RvD1, MaR1, or comparable direct specialized pro-resolving mediator therapy against tissue-repair outcomes.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Watch for direct-SPM human repair, wound, muscle, bone, or regenerative-medicine trials before considering a stage upgrade.

known_gaps.description: Existing human evidence is strongest for SPM bioavailability, leukocyte phenotype, inflammatory cytokines, wound-fluid PMNs, and wound-area trends rather than definitive repair efficacy.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require prespecified repair or healing endpoints, preferably in older or impaired-repair populations, before strengthening public claims.

known_gaps.description: Direct-SPM human safety, chronic dosing, product-specific exposure, and durability remain thinly covered.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Track longer safety, dose-ranging, and durability reports for RvD1, MaR1, or defined SPM products.

known_gaps.description: The current record set does not include a recent systematic review specific to SPMs, EPA/DHA, aging, and human tissue repair.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Consider coverage repair for review-level triangulation only if future public claims move beyond low-confidence biomarker-stage framing.

next_coverage_action: Continue ordinary surveillance for posted NCT03576989 results, direct-SPM human repair studies, and safety/durability evidence. Coverage repair is not required before the next delta pass.
